title: pittosporum bouletii
intro: Pittosporum bouletii, also known as Kohuhu, is a small evergreen tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The leaves of the Pittosporum bouletii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, and have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have also been used in traditional Maori medicine for their therapeutic properties.
